purpose: A single register of what is MISSING, ESTIMATED, or PENDING across the three repos (policy recommendations, digital-twin-for-ipa, EC sub-sector), ranked so the highest-leverage gaps to close are obvious. Separates gaps that are FIXABLE (a known source exists) from STRUCTURAL limits (the data does not exist at this granularity anywhere).

biggest_gap.what: Undeveloped state land banks are quantified for ONLY ONE state (Odisha, 104,026 ac via IDCO ArcGIS). The other 27 industrial states' undeveloped banks are unknown — so the national 'developable' supply tier in the funnel rests on a single data point extrapolated.
biggest_gap.why_it_matters: This is the swing factor in the whole supply-demand verdict. If other states hold Odisha-scale undeveloped banks, 'ready-land scarcity' softens; if they don't, it hardens.
biggest_gap.fix: Pull each state's own land-bank portal (tiered by accessibility below). ~5-6 are machine-readable now.

machine_readable_now.Odisha IDCO: DONE — open ArcGIS REST gis.investodisha.gov.in .../Landbank_Info/MapServer/1/query, per-plot geometry+category+acres
machine_readable_now.UPSIDA (UP): HTML-table, one GET: eservices.onlineupsida.com/PropertyHub/Index.aspx — all plots + PRICE

needs_headless_or_post.MIDC (Maharashtra): AJAX controller returns 411, needs body/headless browser

other_missing.status: BLOCKED — IILB GeoServer WFS disabled (WMS raster only). Without coordinates, no proximity-to-port/highway/power analysis and no map.
other_missing.fix: state ArcGIS endpoints (Odisha proves it's possible) carry geometry; assemble state-by-state

other_missing.status: MISSING for most — IILB returns vacant plots but not rates. Price is what decides whether 'available' land is actually competitive vs the distressed pool.
other_missing.fix: UPSIDA publishes price; RIICO/TGIIC have rate cards; scrape per state

other_missing.gap: Servicing status of undeveloped banks (power/water/road/effluent connectivity)
other_missing.status: ASSUMED, not verified — the funnel's 'needs capex' tier can't yet distinguish serviced-but-unallotted from raw
other_missing.fix: state park brochures + DPR status; no single source

other_missing.status: COUNTS known (1,436 ongoing IBC liquidations, GIDC ~4,450 ac, PSU ~3,400 ac) but most parcels UNSIZED
other_missing.fix: BAANKNET (ibapi.in) auction listings + IBBI liquidation list carry plot area per lot

demand_side_gap.what: Land-intensity benchmarks (Rs cr/acre) exist for only ~2 sector anchors (bulk-drug parks 0.94-1.37, medical-device ~16). That is why the three demand scenarios span 13x — the uncertainty IS the missing benchmark.
demand_side_gap.fix: PARIVESH EC detail records carry PROJECT AREA (ha) AND project cost per proposal — extracting both would give a real Rs cr/acre distribution PER sub-sector, collapsing the demand band from a guess to a measurement. This is the single highest-value pending pull on the demand side.
demand_side_gap.also: State single-window APPROVED-project lists (GO-SWIFT/MAITRI/Guidance-TN/iORA) carry land area + investment per cleared project

structural_limits_unfixable: FDI state series starts Oct-2019 (pre-2019 is by RBI regional office, bundles states) — no earlier state series exists anywhere

FDI attributed to registered office, not project site — a Maharashtra/Delhi FDI figure is bookkeeping location, not where the factory is. Only company-level project data (which India does not publish centrally) could fix this
IEM Part B under-measures services and sub-threshold projects by design
2019 IEM column is DEFECTIVE in the DPIIT source (Gujarat 2019 Part B ~6x all-India) — excluded, not recoverable

fixable_gaps.status: MISSING — DPIIT publishes sector-wise AND state-wise but NOT the cross. So 'which sector's FDI landed in which state' is unknown.
fixable_gaps.fix: RBI regional-office bulletins + state single-window approvals approximate it; no clean central source
DIC (District Industries Centre) registrations; state industrial-dept dashboards

fixable_gaps.status: UNVERIFIED — DPIIT's 'implemented' is self-reported, no commissioned-unit audit
fixable_gaps.fix: MCA-21 active-company filings by industrial PIN + GST registration by industrial address = independent realization proxy

concrete_pending_pulls.status: PENDING — open API floors at 2022. Legacy ASP.NET portals cover it: proposal_status_new1.aspx (2014-2022) and offlineproposal_status.aspx (back to 1994). NOT yet fetched.
concrete_pending_pulls.value: Turns the EC snapshot into an 8-30 year time series — enables year-on-year flow, which the whole register currently lacks

concrete_pending_pulls.status: PENDING — carried in the PARIVESH DETAIL record, not the summary list already pulled
concrete_pending_pulls.value: Directly yields the Rs cr/acre demand benchmark (see land.demand_side_gap) AND lets EC pipeline convert to land demand without IEM at all

concrete_pending_pulls.status: PENDING — timestamps are in detail records
concrete_pending_pulls.value: Clearance-speed by state/sector = a real ease-of-doing-business signal, currently absent

quality_caveats: 'Proposal For' (fresh-vs-reuse) is self-declared; 11% left unclassified rather than forced
Activity code missing on 8,037 proposals (7%) — excluded from sub-sector view

gaps.gap: Annual-report India-mention sweep is a yfinance SAMPLE, not exhaustive
gaps.fix: widen the ticker universe; add non-US listings (Europe/Japan/Korea already cached in the market repo)

gaps.gap: PIB match is TITLE-indexed — proven to miss annexure PDFs, R&D directories, regulator orders (the ministry-check reversal found 8 'quiet' companies actually named)
gaps.fix: already partly done via ministry-publication layer; extend to full-text PIB PDF body search, not just headlines

gaps.gap: Parliament sweep is Lok Sabha 18th only
gaps.status: Rajya Sabha now reachable (rsdoc.nic.in) but not fully swept; earlier Lok Sabhas not pulled
gaps.fix: sweep RS ses_no 259-271 + prior LS terms

gaps.gap: Leads are NOT joined to the IEM/EC register
gaps.status: MISSING — we don't yet know which foreign leads have actually FILED an IEM or EC
gaps.fix: fuzzy-match lead company names against PARIVESH proponent field + DPIIT IEM company list

cross_cutting_absences.detail: Almost everything is a single snapshot (IILB, advertised land, EC status, land funnel). No year-on-year flow anywhere except the FDI/IEM year tables.
cross_cutting_absences.highest_leverage_fix: the pre-2022 EC pull (above) is the cheapest way to add a real multi-year axis

cross_cutting_absences.detail: No jobs data in any layer. IEM carries PROPOSED EMPLOYMENT per memorandum — never extracted.
cross_cutting_absences.fix: DPIIT IEM records include employment intent; pull alongside investment

cross_cutting_absences.detail: Land readiness really turns on distance to port/highway/power/water — not collected. The funnel treats 'developed' as binary.
cross_cutting_absences.fix: needs the per-parcel geometry gap closed first, then overlay PM Gati Shakti / port / NH layers

cross_cutting_absences.detail: Two oilfield files unreadable from ~/Downloads (India_Oilfield_Land_Reuse_Legal_Analysis.docx, India_Acreage_Onshore_Offshore_Split_1.docx) — acreage was independently sourced instead; these remain a cross-check once moved out of ~/Downloads.

top_5_highest_leverage: 1. PARIVESH EC detail records (area+cost per proposal) -> real Rs cr/acre benchmark, collapses the 13x demand band AND lets EC drive land demand directly
2. Pre-2022 EC via legacy portals -> the one cheap way to add a real 2014-2022(+) TIME axis to a register that is otherwise all snapshots
3. The 5-6 machine-readable state land-bank portals (UPSIDA/APIIC/TGIIC/RIICO/HSIIDC) -> generalises the Odisha undeveloped-bank proof from 1 state to ~6, the swing factor in the supply verdict
4. State single-window APPROVED-project lists -> actual realised investment + land area (ground-truths IEM Part B, which is self-reported)
5. Join leads to the IEM/EC proponent register -> tells us which foreign targets have already filed vs are still only 'intent', sharpening the targeting shortlist
note: Structural limits (FDI registered-office attribution, no pre-2019 FDI state series, no central district-level series) are recorded so they are not mistaken for fixable gaps. Everything under 'concrete_pending_pulls', 'machine_readable_now' and 'top_5_highest_leverage' has a known, tested access route.
